title: berkheya lanceolata
intro: Berkheya lanceolata, also known as the yellow daisy bush or yellow hawk's beard, is a flowering plant native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. It is a shrub-like plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Berkheya lanceolata can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
